transcript.text: Hey, how's it going?
I just saw your comment on Slack.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, I was trying to cheat and get one in there right before we started our meeting.
Yeah, I was going to say, I have a weird thing where Slack, like I'll get the update for a message on my phone first before my computer.
Oh, no, it's the opposite of what you want to happen.
Yeah, I know, right?
Someone was like phishing for my phone to see what someone said.
Hi, Steve, how's it going?
Hey, good, how are you?
Good.

Ada, unfortunately, can't make it today, but I put together an agenda that I think will cover everything we sort of need to talk about today, and I'm happy to also capture, like, any questions that I can't answer and route them through Ada and get any answers on Slack as well to make sure we kind of cover everything you want to cover as well.
So, is it just the three of us today?
It is, right?
I think so.
Okay, cool.
So before I jump into any content updates, I just wanted to talk a little bit about moving out of Strategy Sprint and into Phase 2 and also answer any questions you might have.
In terms of the day-to-day operations, not much is going to change.

From your end, I'll be continuing to be the managing editor on your content and sort of I'll be here for meetings and talking through any feedback you have on articles, you know, presenting new assignments.
I think the biggest difference will be instead of Ada, we'll be, we're sort of moving into a pod managed by Jacob, who I have added to our check-in next week.
He'll be joining us to kind of introduce himself and talk to you a little bit about his experience.
But he has done, he has managed like content marketing and SEO for a number of different SaaS and B2B businesses.

He's really deep and great at the SEO strategy and finding edge cases and new opportunities based on performance once we get into it that are some places we might be able to experiment and build growth.
And Ada was sort of under him on his team before she moved over to Strategy Sprint.
So they worked really closely together.
And I think if you like working with Ada, it'll be a great experience working with Jacob as well.
So you're in good hands.
Exciting.
So yeah, he'll be here next week.
So ahead of inviting him to our sync, like I'll meet with him and kind of give him a rundown and get him up to speed.
And then he'll probably have some questions for you guys as well.
Sweet.
Yeah.
So yeah, any questions that you have in the meantime, feel free to ask me or drop on Slack.
Sweet.
Sounds good.
Great.
then in terms of...
of...
So updates for this week.
So we just routed a couple new articles for you to review, which I'm sure you haven't had a chance to get to since we just sent them this morning.
And I saw your feedback, Azzam, I did capture that on the comparison articles, on them being still a little too far in the direction of, like, praising the tools.
So I think we'll dial that back a little bit.
So I think we had those two kind of comparison articles, and then there was two more this week.

So we're going to do, I want to update the writing guidelines, and we'll do one more pass through those, kind of all four of those articles, before we kind of, and so I'll send those to you as a batch as well, to take a look at and make sure we're kind of getting that right.
Great.
Sounds good.
Cool.
Quick update, kind of adjacent, but quick update on our side.
And yesterday we launched support for StoryBlock, so a CMS where, so like all the articles that are live right now are actually being served through StoryBlock.
And so...
Hopefully, kind of the next row of articles, we'll be able to get live without having to go through the whole Google Docs.
Yeah.
Oh, awesome.
Okay.
So, yeah, then in terms of publishing more automatically for you guys, I wasn't sure.
That's probably an ADA question, but do we have, do you know if we have the access we need to your back end to start staging and publishing for you?
I don't think I've, I've invited Azzam.
I don't think I've invited anybody else, but we'll invite whoever we need to.
Okay, great.
So I'll figure out who exactly that would be to make sure we are set up for you.
And then would you like us to kind of stage them or go ahead and publish them?
I'm just thinking in terms of process.
Once you've created them in the Google Doc, do you want us to go straight to publishing or do you want them to be staged?
I think for now, just stage is probably the way that we'll feel the most comfortable.
And as we move along, that might change and like they will, but I'd love to just have the final go ahead and publishing.
go.
Let's Let's you.
Yep.
Awesome.
Okay, so I will figure out who needs access to what so we can get that set up.
Exciting.
Cool.
Yeah, I'm going to get in there myself and figure out how that works.
So hopefully, y'all aren't too far behind after that.
That sounds good.
And I saw, so yeah, so we'll take a look at those comparison articles and get that kind of recalibrated so we're taking the right angle on that kind of whole bucket of content.

And then in terms of the content OS, I was going to show you that we have sort of the 75 new assignments based on keyword research, but it sounds like you were already in there based on your Slack message right before now.
Yeah.
And just for some context, essentially, there's like, I want to say maybe five to 10 that I'd recommend do we not prioritize right now.
Okay.
And those are because there's just specific use cases that I don't think that we're like a great fit for just right now.
And one of them is like kind of around software engineering, like code review and things of that nature, where there is like a specialized subset of AI.
tools that are highly adopted and highly successful right now that I don't think it makes sense for us to go head-to-head with.
I'd rather focus on sort of what we call back-office operations, logistics use cases.
that's like, you know, I see in here as list, we have PO reviews, we have business process optimization, we have SEO stuff for marketing.
I think I'm good with all that stuff.
I just don't want to go head-to-head against software engineering AI tools just yet.
Yeah, that totally makes sense.
And even with that, I think there's a ton of, a different, a ton of content in there that we can get started on.
that sounds good.
And within that, I think there's a few around like PO optimization, like PO review and automation, sorry, and optimization.
And then just like procurement, invoice reconciliation.
These are use cases that are pretty adjacent to what we have with DroneSense right now as an actual customer.
And so I think it makes sense for us to prioritize those and double down in particular, because we have quotes and actual like.
Logic docs we can share and things of that nature.
Awesome.
Is that something that you commented on in Airtable or would you like me to pull those out?
commented on at least one in Airtable with that specific kind of direction, but not on, I didn't see a whole ton around like other things aside from PO reconciliation.
So I wanted to put that as a general note to you all.
Okay, awesome.
I will look and see if there's anything else in there that fits that.
And if not, we can kind of prioritize that as well for the next batch of new assignments.
Yeah, sweet.
Thank you.
Awesome.
And then I did want to, I saw the comments you left on the article that dealt with the garmentory numbers.

And normally I would try to dig into this on our side, but Kavishka, who built the artifacts and who sort of works in the kind of creating the content within our pipeline, is actually out with a family emergency this week.
So I wanted to run it by you to not hold us up, but because I think also because...
Because I sort of joined late in this sprint.
remember looking into it that we sort of had the case study you shared.
And then we also had the company context artifact that you all reviewed.
And I realized that they have different numbers.
And so I wanted to make sure that that was something, like, I wasn't sure, essentially, if the study had So I had the chance to bounce this off, Steve, before our call just now.
And it looks like those are, like, legit numbers.
Okay.
I think what's happening is that there was some context that Steve shared with you all before I joined as well that is, like, accurate and meaningful and important and correct.
It's just, like, there's also a separate case study that I helped produce after joining that doesn't include all those same numbers.
Which we have as well.
Okay.
Yeah, absolutely.
So is it okay to use both sets of these numbers or would you sort of rather we pull these, like, more specific numbers out of the content?
Okay.
I want to say it's fine, Steve.
What's your take on this?
Yeah, I mean, they're legit numbers.
I think it's fine to use.
Yeah.
Okay, great.
So then we'll keep them in, and then that's just good context.
I just want to be doubly sure that nothing got hallucinated.
Thank you for verifying.
One thing I would avoid, maybe I have mixed feelings about Elise, say, is like talking about the contractors going from four to zero.
Like I would frame it as like they didn't have to hire additional contractors, not as like they fired their human workforce.
Yeah, yeah.
And there's just like negative subtext to that sort of framing.
Yeah, I think that makes a lot of sense.
And we will, yeah, we'll update it there.
But yeah, that's a great flag.
And we'll be careful of that.
The way I like to always try to frame it is that you can scale your operations without scaling hiring in the same proportion.
So it's like, yeah, sort of multiplying effect.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay, so for content updates, that was all I had on the docket for today.
Was there any other sort of feedback you had, either granular or sort of, you know, big picture on either the assignment topics or like the actual articles that you've been reviewing?
No, I feel good.
And I am, I feel that the amount of feedback I'm leaving is like slightly less than it was just a week or two ago.
So I feel good about that.
Hopefully I can be less and less involved and just kind of flag any, if there happen to be any just like major issues.
But overall, I feel pretty solid.
I did have a couple of questions that are like not quite related to this stream of work that I wanted to get y'all's take on.
Okay.

One of them is, so we've been talking internally about in addition to like SEO focused evergreen content, producing sort of just thought leadership opinionated content that is like kind of more moment in time.
That might, you know, get us some engagement attraction on LinkedIn.
Then
Other sources like that.
We plan to create and run that internally.
I guess my question is, do you often work with folks that do stuff like this, if or how you might want to be involved?
I'm curious what your take is on us doing something like that.
Yeah.
I know.
So I'm pretty new at GrowthX.

So I personally haven't worked on it, but I know that for clients who similarly sort of want to create a different stream of not SEO content, but more thought leadership pieces, they have built out like a separate pipeline for that.
But it would be kind of a different engagement and a different pipeline, just because the needs of it are very different.
The inputs are really different.
And generally, we tend not to do social content, like people who want LinkedIn posts.
I think for a few reasons, I think some of it's just because.
We're good at what we do in this lane.
And like social is a very different thing.
It's very voicey.
So, but definitely like thought leadership content is something we have produced for other clients.

And if you're sort of interested in talking about that, then I'm happy to get you some more information and put you in touch with a person who has kind of more background on what we've done for specific clients who would be able to talk in more detail about that.
Okay, cool.
I mean, I think that we feel decent about our ability to produce this internally.
Yeah.
But I'd love to, meanwhile, just get a sense of what, how that might fit into this engagement.
Like, is that something that we can produce as part of this?
Is that going to be a whole separate like stream of like contracting work with y'all?
I want to just get a sense of how that might work.
And if there's like examples of stuff that y'all have helped produce, that gives me a great sense of how that might fit into our strategy as well.
Yeah.
So, let me, let me chase down more info on that for you and I'll get back to you on Slack.
right back.
That's right, Steve, I saw you on mute.
Anything you wanted to add there?
I was going to say, I believe Marcel and I briefly spoke about this early on in engagement.
I do think it's a separate kind of line of work, but that's as far as we got to it.
Yeah, I think in general, that's how it's been treated as like a different engagement and a different line of work because it does require a separate pipeline.
But if you're interested in seeing like more specifically how that's played out with clients, then I can get more info.
Yeah, I mean, I would expect that we wouldn't want to go ahead with that in the near future, but I'd love to just get a sense of what that might look like and have it in our back pocket.
Yeah, for sure.
That makes sense.
I'll get you more info on that.
Cool.

Yeah, then the other thing aside from that, wanted to ask you all was just like, if you have any thoughts on how it's been going so far, anything you want to share with us as far as like feedback and the internal and any any early thoughts or, you know.
Concerns, questions on the numbers, anything like that, I want to hear from you all about that as well.

Yeah, so I touched base with Ada before this, just to take a look at your Looker and see if there's sort of, obviously it's early days, there's four published, but just to kind of pull out some of the signals we're seeing, and I think it like, for so far, it looks like it's on track.
You know, what we're seeing is an increase in both sessions and engaged sessions, a little bit like she talked about when she walked you through the Looker dashboard.
They're sort of proportionally climbing in the way that we'd want to see, and so, you know, there's a rise.

You'll see that there's sort of a rise, not a big rise in clicks, which is something that's sort of an expected pattern for these early days, because as your sort of average position is improving from, you know, page four to page three to page two, it's sort of counting as being seen by Google, but like people don't really scroll down to page two, right?
So it's being seen
But it's not, like, really being seen yet by your audience.
So that, I think, is something that you'll see on Looker.

And if it's not a concern about, like, it's not the right kind of content or it's not the right search intent, it's much more just about, like, when it counts as being seen versus, like, when it's actually seen by a human.
And I saw that there was a big spike in traffic from Product Hunt this week.
So congrats.
Thank you.
We ended it yesterday at number three on Product Hunt, which is pretty nice.
Yeah, that's awesome.
That's really exciting.
Yeah, so I'd say that there's not, like, a huge, anything huge to report.
But, like, there are some of the signs of early progress that you'd expect to see.
That's good to know.

And I'd love, like, over the course of this engagement, for you all to continue just sharing anything that stands out to you, whether it's good or bad, so you can just get a sense of how this is progressing.
I it's nice to get indication for what...
The work that we're putting in is producing.
Yeah, for sure.

And I think often, I'll have to check with Jacob, but I think often the way he likes to work is he will sort of do a bigger, more in-depth kind of like monthly look into the numbers and the trends rather than these kind of like week by week, you know, bite size because you often don't see.
So often like, I'll just take a look at the look or give you like a little bit of the bullet points on a weekly basis of like, here's what we're seeing.

And then I think with a lot of clients, so once a month do more of like a deep dive into like trends we're seeing, like identifying opportunities and like kind of go into sort of the more like meatier parts about reporting on a weekly basis.
So, and then, you know, and if there's a specific way you want to work in that way, like I know he's very open to that too.
So we can for sure talk about that next week, like what that'll look like now that we're picking up Velocity and publishing more, you know, what you would like to see in terms of like reporting.
Okay, cool.
That's awesome.
Thank you.
And I guess one related question, this has come up a little bit in the aftermath of the product launch.
One of the unexpected outcomes of that is we start getting outreach from a bunch of different vendors who are selling various growth and SEO services and things of that nature.
And one of the things that I've seen a couple of times is like, hey, do you all want to help guest posting on other websites to get backlinks?
And people offer to do that at some kind of scale.
Is that something that you would recommend generally we make a conscious part of our strategy is like go in and try to procure backlinks?
I know that was a thing in SEO like five or 10 years ago.
I don't know to what extent it's going to be.
Yeah.
I mean, to me, I always think of, mean, there's obviously there's like the like white hat strategies to do it.
There's like the more gray hat strategies to do it.
But that's a question that I would rather ask Ada since she has a better sense of, since she's been at GrowthX for so much longer in terms of how.
she's lot of

Backlinks have worked kind of at this scale, and if that's something that she would recommend looking into, or if that's something that we also do, because it's not something that I've done for clients here, but that doesn't mean that it's something that we haven't done since I've only been here for a month.
Yeah, for sure.
So let me ask her what she thinks about that, and I'll get back to you on that as well.
Okay, sweet.

And just to be clear, my question isn't specifically about, like, using one of these, like, random hawks on, like, post-product-up launch, but, like, should we, even if it's, like, myself, would you all make that part of our strategy, or is that something that we just expect to happen organically, and we just sort of let do its thing and not worry about?
Yeah.
Yeah.

Oh, and I also wanted to say, in terms of content updates, one thing that I think we should start doing now that we're getting publishing is kind of updating your content on a regular basis to make sure that the internal links are kind of in place, too, since as we're publishing more and more content.
I think, like, especially in the first couple articles we published, there's, like, a lot of opportunities there for linking internally to new blog posts.
So we'll also work on sort of updating that on a regular basis as well on our end.
Great.
And that's an area where I expect it'll be quite helpful for you all to have access directly to posts.
Yes, exactly.
That'll be, like, hugely helpful.
So once we are sort of in, yeah, in your sort of more CMS, then we'll be able to do that kind of just automatically without having to loop you guys in.
Sweet.
Okay, cool.
Hopefully we can do that soon rather than later then.
Sweet.
Yes.
Awesome.

Okay, so I will follow up with Ada and just asking her about the backlinks, if that's something we should just wait to happen organically, or if there are things we can or should be doing to kind of accelerate that, especially for our clients who sort of don't have as much of a blog presence when we're getting started.
Yeah.
Sweet.
Okay.
Thank you.
Awesome.
Is there any other questions you had this week?
No, that's it for me.
All right.
All
All right.
Well, thank you.
And have a great week and we'll talk to you next week.
Thanks.
too.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on Phase 2 transition, content feedback, and new processes.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=28.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Phase 2 Transition:** The project is moving to a new pod led by Jacob (SEO/SaaS expert), replacing Ada. Bailey remains the managing editor.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=61.0)
  - [**New Publishing Workflow:** Logic's new StoryBlock CMS enables direct staging by GrowthX. Logic will retain final publishing approval for now.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=227.0)
  - [**Content Strategy Refined:** Focus shifts from software engineering AI to Logic's core strengths (back-office ops, logistics), prioritizing topics adjacent to the DroneSense case study.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)
  - [**Performance:** Early metrics show positive trends (sessions, engagement). A low click-through rate is expected, as articles are improving search rank but are not yet on page one.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=825.0)

## Topics

### Phase 2 Transition & New Pod Lead

  - [The project is transitioning from the Strategy Sprint to a new pod led by Jacob.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=61.0)
  - [**Jacob's Background:** Deep SEO and content marketing experience with SaaS/B2B companies.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Jacob's expertise in finding "edge cases" and new growth opportunities aligns with the project's goals.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=111.0)
  - [**Continuity:** Bailey remains the managing editor. Jacob previously managed Ada, ensuring a smooth handover.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [**Next Step:** Jacob will join next week's sync for introductions.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)

### Content Strategy & Prioritization

  - [**Comparison Articles:** Feedback noted the tone is too "praising."](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**Action:** Bailey will update writing guidelines and revise the four comparison articles for a more neutral, balanced perspective.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
  - [**Content OS (Airtable):**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)
      - [**Deprioritized:** \~5–10 topics on software engineering/code review AI.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Avoids direct competition with established, specialized tools.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
      - [**Prioritized:** Topics on back-office ops, logistics, and procurement (e.g., PO review, invoice reconciliation).](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Adjacent to the DroneSense customer case study, providing access to real-world examples and quotes.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
  - [**Garmentory Case Study:**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)
      - [**Issue:** Discrepancy between two sets of Garmentory numbers in source documents.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)
      - [**Resolution:** Both sets of numbers are legitimate and can be used.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)
      - [**Framing Correction:** Reframe the "4 contractors → 0" statistic.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=559.0)
          - [**Problem:** Implies job elimination.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=559.0)
          - [**Solution:** Frame as "scaling operations without proportional hiring," focusing on efficiency gains.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=559.0)

### New Publishing Workflow (StoryBlock)

  - [Logic's new StoryBlock CMS enables a more efficient publishing workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=227.0)
  - [**Process:** GrowthX will stage articles directly in StoryBlock. Logic will retain final approval for publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=227.0)
  - [**Action:** Bailey will identify the required GrowthX team members for StoryBlock access.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)

### Performance & Reporting

  - [**Early Metrics (Looker):**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=825.0)
      - [**Positive Signals:** Sessions and engaged sessions are increasing proportionally.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=844.0)
      - [**Expected Pattern:** Low click-through rate (CTR) is normal. Articles are improving search rank (e.g., page 4 → 3), but aren't yet on page one where most clicks occur.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=844.0)
      - [**Traffic Spike:** A recent Product Hunt launch drove significant traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=919.0)
  - [**Reporting Cadence:**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=962.0)
      - [**Weekly:** Bailey will provide high-level updates.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=962.0)
      - [**Monthly:** Jacob will lead deeper dives into trends and opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=962.0)
      - [**Note:** The team can discuss and adjust this cadence next week.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=962.0)

### Other Strategic Questions

  - [**Thought Leadership Content:**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
      - [**Inquiry:** Can Logic produce thought leadership content (e.g., LinkedIn posts) via this engagement?](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
      - [**GrowthX Response:** This is a separate service requiring a different pipeline. GrowthX can provide info/examples for future consideration.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [**Backlink Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=1023.0)
      - [**Inquiry:** Should Logic proactively build backlinks (e.g., guest posting) or rely on organic growth?](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=1023.0)
      - [**GrowthX Response:** Bailey will consult Ada for guidance on effective backlink strategies for new blogs.](https://fathom.video/share/4u5Zf9hMoAQBkoqzXhnJ1VnH_pENt_p9?tab=summary&timestamp=1023.0)

## Next Steps
